picks.reason: McLean's AGG profile (xFIP 3.24, K-BB 19.4%) is strong and Lodolo's seasonal ERA overstates his decline (AGG SIERA 3.76), so this is roughly two comparable arms. NYM at -133 is near my fair number with their weak vs-RHP wRC+ of 89 offsetting the edge. No gap.

Avila (AGG xFIP 6.04, K-BB -1.5%) is genuinely dreadful and Littell is a contact-prone fly-baller in a hitter park, so I lean Over the 10. But WAS at -130 already prices in the pitching gap, and KC's bullpen is shaky enough that the side has no value. No qualifying edge.

Alcantara's AGG (xFIP 4.19) plus a dominant L14 (3.26 xFIP, 0.8 BB/9 over 22 IP) is trending up, while Painter's 6.43 ERA reflects real struggles backed by AGG SIERA 4.78 and weak K-BB 9.1%. The market shaded toward PHI on name value, but I make MIA closer to a coin flip; at -102 (51% implied) my estimate is ~56%. Fairbanks closer concern noted but game projects competitive.
picks.data_gap: confirmed lineups would firm this read

picks.reason: Mize's 2.27 ERA is a regression mirage (AGG xFIP 3.91/SIERA 3.85), and as the road side at -110 I have no edge backing him. Lambert similarly outperforming his AGG xFIP 4.43. Two regression candidates priced near pick'em — no actionable side.
SD starter is TBD — automatic pass per data integrity rules.

Ohtani (AGG xFIP 2.98, K-BB 23.3%) vs McClanahan (AGG xFIP 3.69) is a strong pitching duel and LAD at -182 is fairly priced for a dominant home team. No value on the heavy chalk and the RL at +117 still requires a two-run win against a sharp TB club. Lean Under the lowered 7.

Aldegheri's AGG profile is alarming (xFIP 6.07, SIERA 5.69, K-BB just 2.3%) — his 2.12 ERA over 17 IP is pure luck and a fade signal. E-Rod is the steadier arm (AGG SIERA 4.58) at home, and AZ's lineup (118 vs LHP, Carroll 216) is markedly better. Market at -167 (62.5% no-vig ~64%) undersells Aldegheri's true-talent gap; I'm ~68%.

Scherzer's recent line is catastrophic but his AGG xFIP 4.90 keeps true talent murky, and Bennett is a small-sample arm too (15.1 IP). Both starters carry [sm] flags on recent data, leaving the price near pick'em with too much noise to bet confidently.

Rodón's AGG (xFIP 3.94, K-BB 15.8%) is solid vs Kay (AGG xFIP 4.64) and NYY is the better team, but -186 fully prices that. CHW's strong vs-LHP wRC+ of 128 actually argues against laying this much chalk. RL at +110 tempting but no clean edge.
SF starter is TBD — automatic pass.

Gavin Williams (AGG xFIP 3.77, K-BB 15.3%) is clearly superior to Sproat (5.70 ERA, AGG xFIP 4.28), but MIL is the far better team at home with a red-hot offense, and the market correctly has CLE as a near-coinflip dog despite the pitching edge. My number lands within 3 pts of the line.

Sullivan has only 3 career IP — pure small sample, no reliable anchor — so I won't build a side on it. Assad's AGG (xFIP 4.36) is unremarkable. CHC at -187 is steep chalk I can't price with confidence given the unknown COL arm.

Ashcraft's AGG profile is excellent (xFIP 3.27, SIERA 3.35, K-BB 20.0%) and significantly better than Civale (AGG xFIP 4.97, SIERA 4.84, weak K-BB). In a high-variance hitter park the better-arm edge compresses, but PIT at -120 (54.5%) undervalues a genuine starter mismatch; I'm ~59%. Lean Over given the 121 runs park factor.

Kirby's AGG (xFIP 3.37, K-BB 18.4%) edges Bradish (AGG xFIP 3.72) and SEA's pitcher-park suppresses runs, but SEA at -143 with a 5.92-ERA closer in Muñoz prices this fairly. BAL's strong vs-RHP lineup (Rutschman, Alonso) keeps it close. Lean Under the 7.5 in this run-suppressing park.
